title: Eddy — fluid studio
description: A real-time, from-scratch incompressible Navier–Stokes AND magnetohydrodynamics studio in pure TypeScript. Stable Fluids with semi-Lagrangian + MacCormack advection and FOUR pressure-projection Poisson solvers over one identical Neumann/obstacle stencil — red-black SOR, matrix-free Jacobi-preconditioned Conjugate Gradients, a from-scratch geometric MULTIGRID V-cycle (work-optimal O(N), grid-independent convergence), and MULTIGRID-PRECONDITIONED CG (MGCG) — plus OPEN inflow/outflow boundaries that turn the tank into a true channel so a von Kármán wake leaves the domain instead of recirculating. NEW — a full MAGNETOHYDRODYNAMICS pillar: an in-plane magnetic field coupled to the flow through the LORENTZ FORCE (the divergence-free magnetic tension (B·∇)B, the magnetic pressure absorbed into the velocity's own projection) and the INDUCTION EQUATION (semi-Lagrangian transport + field-line stretching (B·∇)u + Ohmic resistivity), kept solenoidal (∇·B = 0) by the very same Hodge projection that keeps the flow incompressible — no monopoles, no new linear algebra. It carries the canonical plasma physics: ALFVÉN WAVES whose dispersion ω = v_A·k is timed live against the closed form, the ORSZAG–TANG vortex steepening into thin CURRENT SHEETS, magnetic RECONNECTION at a Harris current sheet, and field-line stretching (flux-freezing). Carries vorticity confinement, a Boussinesq temperature field driving real thermal convection, a reactive combustion model with variable-density buoyancy, RGB ink with its own Schmidt-number diffusivity, and arbitrary solid obstacles. A flagship LAGRANGIAN-COHERENT-STRUCTURE (FTLE) render mode integrates the flow map to expose the hidden transport skeleton. Twenty scenes — the von Kármán vortex street (closed AND open-channel), Rayleigh–Bénard convection, a buoyant plume, Kelvin–Helmholtz shear, the lid-driven cavity, a self-sustaining Fire, the Taylor–Green vortex, decaying AND continuously-forced 2-D turbulence, the double-shear-layer benchmark, plus the Orszag–Tang vortex, magnetic reconnection, an Alfvén wave and a magnetized shear layer — plus a live SPECTRA LAB running a from-scratch 2-D FFT to plot the kinetic-energy spectrum E(k), its k^-3/k^-5/3 cascade slopes, AND the spectral energy flux Π(k). Dye/speed/vorticity/pressure/temperature/LCS plus |B|/current-jz/B-field-line views, animated Line Integral Convolution, schlieren and Q-criterion visualisation, streamline + tracer overlays, a live hover probe, WebM capture, and a 49-check numerical verification suite (incl. grid-independent multigrid, MGCG, analytic diffusion-decay and FTLE strain rates, FFT/Parseval identities, exact energy-transfer conservation, the open-channel through-flow, AND the MHD claims: ∇·B cleaning, the Alfvén dispersion relation, ideal-MHD energy conservation, flux-freezing and the Orszag–Tang current-sheet benchmark) that proves the solver's claims live in the browser.
